titleStmt.title: The Ambassador in Great Britain ( (Davis) ) to the Acting Secretary of State

div.head: The Ambassador in Great Britain ( Davis ) to the Acting Secretary of State
div.p:
676. Your 727 January 18th, noon.Not found in Department files. I understand that the attitude of the British Government towards the completion of the International Consortium now under discussion, has not in the past been favorable. It had been thought here that, owing to the inclusion of industrial loans in the proposed agreement, the American group would consist of industrial as well as financial institutions and that
this would result in a monopoly of all business both financial and industrial. Foreign Office did not favor joining a consortium which might involve support of a monopoly which they could not justify. Morgan, who I understand will take up this matter in Paris also next week, has explained here that there was no thought of such monopoly but that the plan now under consideration has to do with financial affairs only. This explanation seems to have given satisfaction and it is hoped the matter will now receive favorable consideration in Great Britain.

Hukuang loan belonging to the original four powers, United States, Great Britain, France and Germany, feeling here is that the transference of German interests to Japan should only be offered to Japanese as part of the whole transaction and should be made as a concession and part of the advantage Japan would receive from coming into the consortium.See telegram of Jan. 4 to the Minister in China, p. 567.
